missed_changes: Strong embrace of OPTIMISTIC rollups and the Optimism/OP Stack ecosystem specifically — built and championed the Magi OP Stack client and repeatedly boosted Optimism (🟠), plus EIP-4844/proto-danksharding and the broad L2 landscape; the prediction had framed scaling chiefly through ZK rollups.
Broad AGI/general-AI commentary beyond the AI×crypto thesis (AGI-timeline takes like 'AGI before Mars', 'machines from toys to gods', stacking-sigmoids model of progress).

Vocal open-source-AI advocacy not in the prediction: criticizing OpenAI's increasingly 'equivocating' closed models and Meta's restricted LLaMA release, arguing weights should be public ('open source is the only way to preserve the flame').
New policy/geography concern: the U.S. losing its web3 developer lead and needing regulatory clarity, plus the idea that well-designed protocol rules can reduce the need for regulation.
New emphasis on formal verification / smart-contract security tooling (Halmos symbolic testing, Auction Zoo, etc.) and client-diversity advocacy.

Newly vocal pro-Bitcoin-improvement stance — advocating bringing ZK to Bitcoin and adding a VERIFY_SNARK opcode to make BTC programmable, plus warning its security model must change; the prediction was entirely ETH-centric and ignored Bitcoin.

Strong enthusiasm for optimistic rollups and the OP Stack/Optimism/Base ecosystem (built and released the Magi OP Stack client) — a broader L2 embrace than the predicted 'ZK rollups as the predominant compute layer.'
New excitement for on-chain IP / Story Protocol (an on-chain remix/attribution graph, 'multiplayer for IP') as a sector not anticipated in the prediction.

The crypto+AI angle he actually pursued was deepfake-resistance / content provenance (ZK proofs of image provenance, attestation graphs, AGI commentary), not the predicted 'autonomous self-owning programs.'
Positive engagement with proof-of-personhood (Worldcoin/World ID as complement to social-graph approaches) and account abstraction / 'smart accounts' within the identity theme.
Adopted a terminology shift, replacing 'Data Availability' with 'Data Publishing / Data Storage.'

New strong bitcoin emphasis not anticipated by the Ethereum-centric prediction: advocating ZK/SNARKs 'back home to bitcoin', a VERIFY_SNARK opcode to make bitcoin programmable, and arguing bitcoin's security model must change.
Shipped Lasso (new lookup argument ~10x faster than halo2) and Jolt — an explicitly ISA-/chain-agnostic zkVM (EVM, RISC-V, MIPS), a concrete major SNARK launch broader than Ethereum.

Deepfakes / AI-content provenance became a major new applied focus for crypto+ZK (long July 22 thread, Dec 17), shifting AI+crypto toward provenance/anti-fakery rather than the 'autonomous 0-person protocol' framing in the prediction.
Championed reframing 'Data Availability' as 'Data Publishing'/'Data Storage' — a new terminological/conceptual stance.
Took a notably favorable, defended stance on Worldcoin / World ID biometric proof-of-personhood as a complement to social-graph approaches.
Broadened beyond Ethereum L2: openness to app-specific chains, alternative VMs (Move, Rust) and MEV internalization (Sept 19 podcast), plus exploring Aleo's Leo private-chain stack.
